title: senecio squalidus subsp. microglossus
intro: Senecio squalidus subsp. microglossus, also known as the 'Old Man in the Mountain' or 'Greek thistle', is a biennial or perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Senecio squalidus subsp. microglossus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of asthma, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Senecio squalidus subsp. microglossus can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
